{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- family crest image -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = Unknown
|家祖 = {{ill|Funase-no-Sukune|ja|船瀬足尼}}
|種別 = [[Shinbetsu]] ({{ill|Tenshin|ja|天津神・国津神|lt=Tenshin}})
|出身地 = [[Yamato Province|Yamato]]
|根拠地 = Kuji Province (later {{ill|Kuji District, Ibaraki|ja|久慈郡|lt=Kuji District}} of [[Hitachi Province]])
|人物 =
|支流 = (kabane in parentheses)<br />{{ill|Tōtsuōmi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|遠淡海国造}}<br />{{ill|Suruga no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|珠流河国造}}<br />{{ill|Kanasashi clan|ja|金刺氏}}<br />{{ill|Kunu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|久努国造}}<br />{{ill|Kuno clan|ja|久野氏|lt=Kunu clan}} (Atai)<br />{{ill|Saya clan|ja|佐夜氏}} (Atai)<br />{{ill|Ochi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|小市国造}}<br />{{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|lt=Ochi clan}} (Atai)<br />{{ill|Kazahaya no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|風速国造}}<br />{{ill|Kazahaya clan|ja|風速氏}} (Atai)
}}
{{nihongo|'''Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|久自国造|''kuji no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kuji kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled the region that later became the northern-central part of [[Hitachi Province]] ('''[[#Territory|Kuji Province]]''').

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
"Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko" is the form given in the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' as recension-edited by the [[Shinto priest]] {{ill|Watarai Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|lt=Watarai Nobuyoshi}} (''Gōtō Kujiki'')<ref name="taikei">''[[#taikei|Kokushi Taikei, vol. 7]]''.</ref><ref name="taikei2">''[[#taikei2|Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei, vol. 7]]''.</ref> and in the same work's [[Andei|Andei]]-era ancient manuscript held by the Maeda Marquess family{{refnest|group="note"|name=maeda|The head of the [[Maeda clan#Toshiie Maeda line|Maeda line of Toshiie]] of the [[Maeda clan]], head of the [[Kaga Domain]], was made a [[Marquess#Japan|marquess]] after the [[Meiji Restoration]].<ref>''Kansei Chōshū Shokafu'', ''Kanazawa City History'', ''Hanshi Daijiten''.</ref>}}.<ref name="taikei2"/> In the same work's [[Jingū Bunko]] text, it is written "'''Ku<span style="-webkit-text-emphasis: filled circle;">me</span><ref>Kume-gō, {{ill|Kume Village, Ibaraki|ja|久米村 (茨城県)|lt=Kume Village}}.</ref> no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''".<ref name="taikei2"/>

=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'', during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]], '''{{ill|Funase-no-Sukune|ja|船瀬足尼}}''' (''Funase-no-Sukune''), a third-generation descendant of '''{{ill|Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto|ja|伊香色雄|lt=Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto}}''' — ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan]] — was appointed first Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko. Funase-no-Sukune is also written '''Funase-no-Shukune'''<ref>[http://www.edu.pref.ibaraki.jp/board/bunkazai/ken/shiseki/12-27/12-27.html Bontenzan Kofun Cluster] — [[Ibaraki Prefectural Board of Education]] (viewed 5 October 2016, 12:12 [[JST]]).</ref> or '''Funase-no-Sukune''' (船瀬足尼).<ref>[http://www.kanko-hitachiota.com/page/page000063.html Bontenzan Kofun Cluster] — Hitachiōta City Tourism and Products Association official website (viewed 16 May 2017, 00:30 JST).</ref> The form "船瀬足尼" is that used in Watarai Nobuyoshi's recension of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi''<ref name="taikei"/><ref name="taikei2"/> and in the Andei-era ancient manuscript held by the Maeda Marquess family,<ref group="note" name="maeda"/><ref name="taikei2"/> while the Jingū Bunko text has "'''Funasene'''" (船瀬尼),<ref name="taikei2"/> which is considered a scribal error.

=== Clan ===

== Base ==
The later '''{{ill|Kuji District, Ibaraki|ja|久慈郡|lt=Kuji District}}''' of Hitachi Province, corresponding to present-day [[Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi]] and [[Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] — the area around {{ill|Inamura Shrine (Hitachiōta)|ja|稲村神社 (常陸太田市)|lt=the Inamura Shrine mentioned below}}. The district headquarters ({{ill|gunga|ja|郡衙}}) of Kuji District is thought to be the '''{{ill|Chōjayashiki Ruins|ja|長者屋敷遺跡}}''', in the Ōsato and Yakuya neighborhoods of Hitachiōta City.

=== Territory ===
The territory of the Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko was the region then known as '''Kuji Province'''. Kuji Province covered the northern-central part of later [[Hitachi Province]] under the [[ritsuryō provinces|ritsuryō system]] ({{ill|Kuji District, Ibaraki|ja|久慈郡|lt=Kuji District}}),<ref name="nihonjiten">[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263233.html Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko (Hitachi)] — Nihon Jiten (viewed 25 February 2018, 03:10 JST).</ref> namely the [[Kuji River, Ibaraki|Kuji River]] basin,<ref>''[[#chizu|Nihon Rekishi Chizu, Primitive and Ancient, Part 2]]''.</ref> corresponding to the area around [[Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] and [[Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta City]] in [[Ibaraki Prefecture]].<ref name="nihonjiten"/>

For the origin of the place name, see {{ill|Kuji District, Ibaraki#Origin of the district name|ja|久慈郡#郡名の由来}}.

Kuji Province was later united, along with six neighboring provinces ({{ill|Tsukuba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑波国造|lt=Tsukuba}}, {{ill|Niihari no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|新治国造|lt=Niihari}}, {{ill|Ibaraki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|茨木国造|lt=Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造|lt=Naka}}, {{ill|Taka Province|ja|高国|lt=Taka (Taka Province)}}, and {{ill|Michinokuchi no Kihe Province|ja|道口岐閉国|lt=Michinokuchi no Kihe Province}}), to form Hitachi Province with the establishment of the ritsuryō provinces.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
The {{ill|ujigami|ja|氏神}} of the Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko is '''{{ill|Inamura Shrine (Hitachiōta)|ja|稲村神社 (常陸太田市)|lt=Inamura Shrine}}''' (''Inamura Jinja''; written either 稲村神社 or 稻村神社; {{Coord|36|31|39.33|N|140|30|03.63|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|name=Inamura Shrine}}) in [[Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta City]], Ibaraki Prefecture (former Kuji District). Its chief enshrined deity is [[Nigihayahi|Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto]], ancestor of the first kuni no miyatsuko's ancestor Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto. According to shrine tradition, when the first kuni no miyatsuko, Funase-no-Sukune-no-Mikoto, was appointed during the reign of Emperor Seimu, he enshrined his great ancestor Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto.

=== Related Shrines ===
* {{ill|Izumi Shrine (Hitachi)|ja|泉神社 (日立市)|lt=Izumi Shrine}} (''Izumi Jinja''; {{Coord|36|31|6.79|N|140|37|29.67|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|name=Izumi Shrine}})
*: A shrine in [[Hitachi, Ibaraki|Hitachi City]] (formerly of Kuji District, later {{ill|Taga District|ja|多賀郡|lt=Taga District}}), Ibaraki Prefecture. It enshrines {{ill|Amenohayatamahime-no-Mikoto|ja|天速玉姫命}} and is identified with the {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|式内小社|lt=shikinaisha of minor rank}} "Amenohayatamahime-no-Mikoto Jinja". According to the shrine's account of its origin ([http://izumi-jinja.sakura.ne.jp/Yuisyo-Page.html Shrine Introduction] — Izumi Shrine official website<ref>Viewed 7 December 2017, 21:49 JST.</ref>), it is said to have been enshrined in the area in "Uji 49" ([[42 BC]])<ref group="note">The meaning of "Uji" is unclear. If "Sujin 49" is meant, mechanically converting the year given in the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'' gives [[49 BC]].</ref> of the reign of [[Emperor Sujin]] (the 10th [[emperor of Japan]]). It also states: "In response to a petition from Funase-no-Shukune, Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko, the [[Ōmi (rank)|minister]] Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto received an imperial decree, came to Kuji Province, enshrined Amenohayatamahime-no-Mikoto, and made her the general tutelary of Kuji Province — thus founding Izumi Shrine." If this were historical, then Funase-no-Shukune and Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto, who lived three generations earlier, would have been alive at the same time. Moreover, the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' states that Funase-no-Sukune's appointment as Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko occurred in the reign of Emperor Seimu (the 13th emperor), so it is inconsistent with the tradition that Funase-no-Shukune was already "Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko" during the reign of Emperor Sujin (the 10th emperor). Regarding Ikagashikoo-no-Mikoto, the "[[Tennō Hongi]]" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' states that he became minister in the 2nd month of [[Emperor Kaika]] 8 — Kaika being the 9th emperor — so, assuming he was not subsequently dismissed, he would still have been minister in the reign of Emperor Sujin.

=== Tomb ===
* '''{{ill|Bontenzan Kofun|ja|梵天山古墳}}''' (''Bontenzan Kofun''; {{Coord|36|30|33.98|N|140|29|32.43|E|region:JP-08_type:landmark|name=Bontenzan Kofun}})
*: A [[keyhole-shaped kofun]] in {{ill|Shima-machi, Hitachiōta|ja|島町_(常陸太田市)|lt=Shima-machi}} (former Kuji District), [[Hitachiōta, Ibaraki|Hitachiōta City]], Ibaraki Prefecture, and the principal tumulus of the {{ill|Bontenzan Kofun Cluster|ja|梵天山古墳群}}. Its mound is 151 m long,<ref>Onsite explanatory signage.</ref> making it the second-largest in Ibaraki Prefecture. The interred figure is traditionally held to be [[#Ancestry|Funase-no-Sukune]].

== People ==
* '''Funase-no-Sukune''' (''Funase-no-Sukune'')
*: First Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko, during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]].

== Notes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Footnotes ===
{{Reflist|group="note"}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist|2}}

== References ==
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]).
* Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Ujizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (Yoshikawa Kōbunkan).
* ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]).
* ''Shintō Daijiten'' (Rinsen Shoten).
* {{Cite book|author=|authorlink=|editor=[[Keizai Zasshi-sha]]|title=Kokushi Taikei, vol. 7|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/991097|accessdate=2017-12-25|year=1901|publisher=[[Keizai Zasshi-sha]]|isbn=|pages=Preface p. 1, main text p. 409|chapter=|ref=taikei}} Link to the [[National Diet Library Digital Collections]].
* {{Cite book|author=|authorlink=|editor=[[Kuroita Katsumi]]|title=[[Kokushi Taikei#Third series: Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei|Shintei Zōho Kokushi Taikei]], vol. 7|edition=new ed.|year=1998|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=4642003088|pages=Sendai Kuji Hongi Preface p. 1, Preface p. 4, main text p. 144|chapter=|ref=taikei2}}
* {{Cite book|author=|editor={{ill|Kondō Toshitaka|ja|近藤敏喬|lt=Kondō Toshitaka}}|title=Kodai Gōzoku Keifu Shūran|year=1993|publisher=[[Tokyodo Publishing]]|isbn=4-490-20225-3|page=73–74|chapter=|ref=keizu}}
* {{Cite book|author=|editor=[[Takeuchi Rizō]] et al.|title=[[Nihon Rekishi Chizu]], Primitive and Ancient, Part 2|year=1982|publisher=[[Kashiwa Shobō]]|isbn=|page=290|chapter=|ref=chizu}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:くしのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:日本の歴史関連のスタブ項目]]
[[Category:常陸国]]
[[Category:茨城県の歴史]]
[[Category:物部氏|-くしのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:久自国造]]
